title: Vitalik Buterin Shares Top Priorities for New 'Lean Ethereum' Strawmap
summary: Vitalik Buterin has outlined priorities for a new 'Lean Ethereum' strawmap, focusing on privacy and scalability. The plan includes a new virtual machine with leanISA and RISC-V as leading candidates.
body:
Vitalik Buterin, co-founder of Ethereum, has shared his top priorities for a new 'Lean Ethereum' strawmap. This project is part of the Ethereum Foundation's broader plan to enhance privacy and scalability on the network. The core idea is to introduce a new virtual machine that could streamline Ethereum's operations.

Buterin highlighted leanISA and RISC-V as the top candidates for this new virtual machine. These technologies aim to make Ethereum more efficient and capable of handling a larger number of transactions. The initiative is still in the early stages, but it represents a significant step toward making Ethereum more user-friendly and scalable.

For everyday users, this could mean faster and more private transactions on the Ethereum network. As the blockchain becomes more efficient, it could also reduce costs and improve the overall user experience. This is particularly important as Ethereum continues to grow in popularity and usage.

The Ethereum Foundation is expected to provide more details on the Lean Ethereum initiative in the coming months. Users should keep an eye on official announcements for updates on how this project will unfold and what it will mean for the future of Ethereum.
